This  is  a  digital  copy  of  a  book  that  was  preserved  for  generations  on  library  shelves  before  it  was  carefully  scanned  by  Google  as  part  of  a  project 

to  make  the  world's  books  discoverable  online. 

It  has  survived  long  enough  for  the  copyright  to  expire  and  the  book  to  enter  the  public  domain.  A  public  domain  book  is  one  that  was  never  subject 

to  copyright  or  whose  legal  copyright  term  has  expired.  Whether  a  book  is  in  the  public  domain  may  vary  country  to  country.  Public  domain  books 

are  our  gateways  to  the  past,  representing  a  wealth  of  history,  culture  and  knowledge  that's  often  difficult  to  discover. 

Marks,  notations  and  other  maiginalia  present  in  the  original  volume  will  appear  in  this  file  -  a  reminder  of  this  book's  long  journey  from  the 

publisher  to  a  library  and  finally  to  you. 

Usage  guidelines 

Google  is  proud  to  partner  with  libraries  to  digitize  public  domain  materials  and  make  them  widely  accessible.  Public  domain  books  belong  to  the 
public  and  we  are  merely  their  custodians.  Nevertheless,  this  work  is  expensive,  so  in  order  to  keep  providing  tliis  resource,  we  liave  taken  steps  to 
prevent  abuse  by  commercial  parties,  including  placing  technical  restrictions  on  automated  querying. 
We  also  ask  that  you: 

+  Make  non-commercial  use  of  the  files  We  designed  Google  Book  Search  for  use  by  individuals,  and  we  request  that  you  use  these  files  for 
personal,  non-commercial  purposes. 

+  Refrain  fivm  automated  querying  Do  not  send  automated  queries  of  any  sort  to  Google's  system:  If  you  are  conducting  research  on  machine 
translation,  optical  character  recognition  or  other  areas  where  access  to  a  large  amount  of  text  is  helpful,  please  contact  us.  We  encourage  the 
use  of  public  domain  materials  for  these  purposes  and  may  be  able  to  help. 

+  Maintain  attributionTht  GoogXt  "watermark"  you  see  on  each  file  is  essential  for  in  forming  people  about  this  project  and  helping  them  find 
additional  materials  through  Google  Book  Search.  Please  do  not  remove  it. 

+  Keep  it  legal  Whatever  your  use,  remember  that  you  are  responsible  for  ensuring  that  what  you  are  doing  is  legal.  Do  not  assume  that  just 
because  we  believe  a  book  is  in  the  public  domain  for  users  in  the  United  States,  that  the  work  is  also  in  the  public  domain  for  users  in  other 
countries.  Whether  a  book  is  still  in  copyright  varies  from  country  to  country,  and  we  can't  offer  guidance  on  whether  any  specific  use  of 
any  specific  book  is  allowed.  Please  do  not  assume  that  a  book's  appearance  in  Google  Book  Search  means  it  can  be  used  in  any  manner 
anywhere  in  the  world.  Copyright  infringement  liabili^  can  be  quite  severe.

CLASS  A.  BOOKS 

Inclodes  books,  pamphlets,  leaflets,  etc.,  and  periodical  contributions,  in  two  groups, 
arranged  alphabetically  by  author  or  title.  The  first  group  contains  the  titles  of 
books  for  which  printed  catalogue  cards  are  issued  by  the  Library  of  Congress,  and 
other  similar  works.  The  second  group  includes  the  titles  of  pamphlets,  leaflets, 
and  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  all  other  productions  registered  under  the 
legal  designation  "book." 

The  number  in  heavy-faced  type  at  the  end  of  a  title  at  the  right  is  a  serial  number 
by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  index  to  this  title.  Thus  in  the  inde^ 
under  the  heading  ' 'Alabama.  Supreme  court,^*  will  be  found  the  number  1. 
A  search  for  this  number  in  the  catalogue  locates  the  title.  This  number  is  used 
instead  of  a  page  number. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  a  title  at  the  left  and  immediately  above  the  copyright 
notice  is  the  Library  of  Congress  order  number  for  the  printed  oard  for  this  title. 
Thus  at  the  end  of  the  title  "Arnold,  Sarah  Louise,  1869,"  is  the  number  6-45339, 
indicating  that  printed  cards  will  be  supplied  by  the  Library  of  Congress,  and 
that  in  ordering  such  cards  this  number  may  be  used.

CLASS  A.  BOOKS 

loclades  )>ookii,  pamphlete,  leaflets,  etc.,  aii<1  periodiml  contribntionn,  in  twoirroap0. 
arraii$ze<l  alphabetically  by  author  or  title.  The  Ar^  Rrrmp  contains  the  title*  oi 
books  for  whi(*h  priuteiil  catalo^e  canls  are  iwneti  by  the  Library  of  Congren.  aiKl 
other  similar  works.  The  pecond  trroap  includen  the  titles  of  pamphlets,  leaflets, 
an^l  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  all  other  productions  rc^stered  under  the 
le^l  cieeitniation  *'book." 

The  number  in  heavy-faced  type  at  the  end  of  a  title  at  the  right  is  a  serial  number 
bv  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  index  to  this  title.  Thus  in  the  index 
under  the  heading  '^Alabama.  Supreme  Court,"  will  be  found  the  numlier  172.  A 
flnan-h  ffir  this  numl>er  in  the  catalogue  locates  the  title.  This  number  is  used 
instead  of  a  page  number. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  a  title  at  the  left  and  immediately  above  the  copyright 
notic^e  is  the  Library  of  Gongrets  ordsr  siimber  for  the  printed  eard  for  this  title. 
Thus  at  the  end  A  the  title  "American  locomotive  eom(»any/'  is  the  numl>er 
6-45747,  indicating  that  printed  canls  will  be  supplie'I  by  the  Library  of  Congress, 
and  that  in  ordering  such  cards  this  number  may  be  used.

CLASS  A.   BOOKS 

Indodes  books,  pamphlets,  leaflets,  eU'..,  and  periodical  con  tri  but  ions,  in  twoj^ronpe. 
arranged  alnhabetuadly  by  author  or  title.  The  first  group  contains  the  titles  of 
books  for  which  printeil  catalogue  cards  are  issued  by  the  Library  of  Congress,  and 
other  similar  works.  The  second  group  Includes  the  titles  of  pamphlets,  leaflets, 
and  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  all  other  productions  roistered  under  the 
l^sal  designation  '*book.'*  The  titles  included  in  the  second  group  will  be  printed 
in  the  last  number  of  the  Catalogue  of  each  month. 

The  number  in  heavy-faced  type  at  the  end  of  a  title  at  the  right  is  a  citation  number 
by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  index  to  this  title.  Thus  in  the  index 
under  the  heading  "Adams,  William  S[idney]  *'  will  be  found  the  number  404.  A 
search  for  this  number  in  the  catalogue  locates  the  title.  This  number  is  used 
instead  of  a  piMte  number. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  a  title  at  the  left  and  immediately  above  the  copyright 
notice  is  the  Library  of  Congresi  order  number  for  the  printed  eard  for  this  title. 
Thus  at  the  end  of  the  title  "Old  saws  with  new  teeth''  is  the  number  7-601, 
indicating  that  printed  cards  will  be  supplied  by  the  Library  of  Congress,  and 
that  in  oraering  such  cards  this  number  may  be  used.

BOOKS;  DRAMATIC  COMPOSITIONS;  MAPS  AND

CLASS  A.  BOOKS 

Includes  bookB,  pamphlets,  leaflets,  etc.,  and  periodical  contributions,  in  two' groups, 
arranged  alphabetically  by  author  or  title.  The  first  group  contains  the  titles  of 
books  for  which  printed  catalogue  cards  are  issued  by  the  Library  of  Congress,  and 
other  similar  works.  The  second  group  includes  the  titles  of  pamphlets,  leaflets, 
and  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  all  other  productions  registered  under  the 
legal  designation  '*lxK)k.'*  The  titles  included  in  the  second  group  will  be  printed 
in  the  last  number  of  the  Catalogue  of  each  month. 

The  number  in  heavy -faced  type  at  the  end  of  a  title  at  the  right  is  a  citation  number 
by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  index  to  this  title.  Thus  in  the  index 
under  the  heading  "Aitken,  Frank  W.,"  will  be  found  the  number  587.  A 
search  for  this  number  in  the  catalogue  locates  the  title.  This  number  is  used 
instead  of  a  page  number. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  a  title  at  the  left  and  immediately  above  the  copyright 

.  notice  is  the  Library  of  CongresB  order  number  for  the  printed  card  for  thii  title. 
Thus  at  the  end  of  the  title  *'A  history  of  the  earthquake  and  fire  in  San  Francisco, 
etc.,''  is  the  number  7-422,  indicating  that  printed  cards  will  be  supplied  by  the 
Library  of  Congress,  and  that  in  ordering  such  cards  this  number  may  be  used.

CLASS  A.  BOOKS 

Includes  booki^,  paniphlete,  leaflet?,  etc.,  and  periodical  con tri hut ionn,  in  tuo  groups, 
arranged  alphabetically  by  author  or  title.  The  first  fnt>op  contains  tiie  titles  of 
books  for  which  printed  catalogue  cards  are  issued  by  the  Lif)rary  of  C'on^rttv,  and 
other  similar  works.  The  second  group  includes  the  titles  of  pan])>hlets,  leaflets, 
and  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  all  other  proiluctions  rc^ist«'re<l  under  the 
legal  designation  "bc<)k."  The  titles  included  in  the  necond  gnnip  willl^e  printtni 
in  the  last  number  of  the  Catalogub  of  each  month. 

The  number  in  heavy-faced  type  at  the  end  of  a  title  at  the  rijurht  is  a  citatitm  numl>er 
by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  index  tu  this  title.  Thus  in  the  index 
under  the  heading  "Adams,  £lizal)eth  Kemper,  aesthetic  exiH'rience,"  will  l>e 
found  the  number  778.  A  search  for  this  number  in  the  catalogue  locates  the 
title.    This  number  is  used  instead  of  a  page  number. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  a  title  at  the  left  and  immediately  al>ove  the  c<ipyright 
notice  is  the  Library  of  Congress  order  mimber  for  the  printed  card  for  thii  title. 
Thus  at  the  end  of  the  title  "The  aesthetic  ext>erience,  etc.,"  is  the  number  7-2424, 
indicating  that  printed  cards  will  be  supplied  by  the  Library  of  Congress,  and  that 
in  ordering  such  cards  this  number  may  be  used.

CLASS  A.  BOOKS 

Includes  books,  pamphlets,  leaflets,  etc.,  and  periodical  contributiooB,  in  two  i^nps. 
arranised  alphabetically  by  author  or  title.  The  iirst  gronp  contains  the  title*  of 
books  for  which  prinieti  catalogue  cards  are  iaaoed  by  the  Library  of  i'ongnsH,  and 
other  (fimilar  works.  The  second  group  includes  the  titles  of  pamphlets,  It^flets, 
and  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  all  other  productions  re^isterKl  under  the 
legal  designation  *'book."  Ihe  titles  included  in  the  second  group  will  be  printed 
in  the  last  number  of  the  Catalog ub  of  each  month. 

The  number  in  heavy-faited  type  at  the  end  of  a  title  at  the  right  is  a  citation  number 
by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  index  to  this  title.  Thus  in  the  index 
nnder  the  heading  "Macmillan  co.,''  will  be  found  the  number  1778.  A  f-ean-h 
lor  this  number  in  the  catalogue  locates  the  title.  This  number  is  used  instead 
of  a  page  number. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  a  title  at  the  left  and  immediately  above  the  copyright 
notice  is  the  Library  of  Congrett  order  nunber  for  the  printed  oard  for  this  title. 
Thus  at  the  end  of  the  title  "Newer  ideals  of  peace,"  is  the  number  7-4377,  indicate 
injs  that  printed  cards  will  be  supplied  by  the  Library  of  Congress,  and  that  in 
ordering  such  cards  this  number  may  be  used.

CLASS  A.  BOOKS 

Inclades  books,  pamphlets,  leaflets,  etc.,  and  periodical  contributions,  in  two  g[roup6, 
arraniired  alphabetically  by  anthor  or  title.  The  first  group  contains  the  titles  of 
books  for  which  printeil  catalogue  cards  are  issued  by  the  Library  of  Congress,  and 
other  similar  works.  The  second  group  includes  the  titles  of  pamphlets,  leaflets, 
and  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  all  other  productions  re^stered  under  the 
legal  designation  ''book/'  The  titles  included  in  the  second  group  will  be  printed 
in  the  last  number  of  the  Catalogue  of  each  month. 

The  number  in  heavy-fai«d  type  at  the  end  of  a  title  at  the  right  is  a  citation  number 
by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  index  to  this  title.  Thus  in  the  index 
under  the  heading  "  New  England  railway  pub.  co.,"  will  be  found  the  number  1948. 
A  search  for  this  number  in  the  catalogue  locates  the  title.  This  number  is  used 
instead  of  a  pafse  number. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  a  title  at  the  left  and  immediately  above  the  copyright 
notice  is  the  Library  of  Congresi  order  namber  for  the  printed  eard  for  thie  title. 
Thus  at  the  end  of  the  title  **  Aeschylus.  Agamemnon,"  is  the  number  7-4797, 
indicating  that  printed  cards  will  be  supplied  by  the  Library  of  Congress,  and  that 
in  ordering  sucn  cards  this  number  may  be  used.

ABC  pathfinder  shipping  and  mailing  guide.    Jan.  1907.        Bos- 
ton, Mass.,  New  England  railway  pub.  co.,  °1906.

CLASS  A.  BOOKS 

Includes  books,  pamphlets,  leafletsi,  etc.,  and  periodical  contributionn,  in  two  groups, 
arranged  alphabetically  by  author  or  title.  The  first  group  containn  the  titles  of 
books  for  which  printed  catalogue  cards  are  issueil  by  the  Library  of  I'ongress,  and 
other  similar  works.  The  second  group  includes  the  titles  of  pamphlets,  leaflets, 
and  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  all  dther  productions  registered  under  the 
legal  designation  '*book."  The  titles  included  in  the  secon<l  grou))  will  l>e  printed 
in  the  last  number  of  the  C  at  a  log  m  of  each  month. 

The  number  in  heavy-tiu^  type  at  the  end  of  a  title  at  the  right  is  a  citation  number 
by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  index  to  this  title.  Thus  in  the  index 
under  the  heading  "West  pub.  co."  will  be  found  the  number  2088.  A  search  for 
this  number  in  the  catalogue  locates  the  title.  This  number  is  used  instead  of  a 
page  number. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  a  title  at  the  left  and  immediately  above  the  copyright 
notice  is  the  Library  of  Congress  order  number  for  the  printed  card  for  thii  title. 
Thus  at  the  end  of  the  title  **  Avey,  Elijah.  The  capture  and  execution  of  John 
Brown,"  is  the  number  7-<>(>42,  indicating  that  printed  cards  will  l>e  supplieii  by 
the  Library  of  Congress,  and  that  in  ordering  such  cards  this  number  may  be 
used.

BOOKS;  DRAMATIC  COMPOSITIONS;  MAPS  AND 

CHARTS 


CLASS  A.  BOOKS 

Inclades  books,  pamphlets,  leaflets,  etc.,  and  periodical  contributions,  in  two  groups, 
arranf^ed  alphabetically  by  author  or  title.  The  first  group  contains  the  titles  of 
books  for  which  printed  catalogue  cards  are  issued  by  the  Library  of  Congress,  and 
other  similar  works.  The  second  group  includes  the  titles  of  pamphlets,  leaflets, 
and  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  all  other  productions  registered  under  the 
legal  designation  **book."  The  titles  included  in  the  second  group  will  be  printed 
in  the  last  number  of  the  Catalogue  of  each  month. 

The  number  in  heavy-fatted  type  at  the  end  of  a  title  at  the  right  is  a  citation  number 
by  menus  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  index  to  this  title.  Thus  in  the  index 
under  the  heading  '^Aegidius  Assiensis"  will  be  found  the  number  2198.  A  search 
for  this  number  in  the  catalogue  locates  the  title.  This  number  is  used  instead 
of  a  page  number. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  a  title  at  the  left  and  immediately  above  the  copyright 
notice  is  the  Library  of  CongreiB  order  number  for  the  printtd  eard  for  tbie  title. 
Thus  at  the  end  of  the  title  **Aegidius  Assiensis,  The  golden  sayings,  etc.,"  is  the 
number  6-46746,  indicating  that  printed  cards  will  be  supplied  by  the  Library  of 
Congress,  and  that  inordering  such  cards  this  number  may  be  used. 

GROUP    1 


Aegidins  Assiensis,  d,  1262. 

The  golden  sayings  of  the  Blessed  Brother  Giles  of  Assisi;  newly 
translated  and  edited,  together  with  a  sketch  of  his  life  by  the  Rev. 
Fr.  Paschal  Uobinson  .  .  .        Philadelphia,  The  Dolphin  press,  1907. 

5  p.  1.,  vii-lxiii,  141  p.     front.,  illus.,  4  pi.     19"».

iBcladas  bookv,  paninhletK,  leafletn,  etc.,  and  periodical  con tribations,  in  two  ^ronps. 
airan^  alphabetically  by  author  or  title.  The  first  group  contains  the  titles  of 
books  for  which  printed  catalogue  cards  are  issaed  by  the  Library  of  Congress,  and 
other  Himilar  works.  The  second  group  includes  the  titles  of  pamphlets,  leaflets, 
and  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  all  other  productions  rc^stered  under  the 
l^a)  dAignation  '*bGK>k."  The  titles  included  in  the  second  group  will  be  printed 
in  the  laj*t  number  of  the  Catalog  ie  of  each  month. 

The  number  in  heavy-faced  type  at  the  end  of  a  title  at  the  ri|(ht  is  a  citation  number 
by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  index  to  this  title.  Thus  in  the  index 
under  the  heading  ^'Adaros  {C  L. )  note8  on  dcKTiptive  geninctry"  will  be  found 
the  number  3253.  A  search  for  this  number  in  the  catalogue  locates  the  title. 
This  nnmber  is  used  instead  of  a  page  numl)er. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  a  title  at  the  left  and  immediately  al)ove  the  copyright 
notice  is  the  Library  of  Coagress  order  nnmbor  for  the  printod  card  for  this  titlo. 
Thus  at  the  end  of  the  title  **Abegg  (Richard  Wilhelm  Heinrich),  The  electrolytic 
dispociation  theory/'  is  the  number  7-6S08,  indicating  that  printed  cards  \%*ill  be 
supplied  by  the  Library  of  Congress,  and  that  in  ordering  mich  cards  this*  number 
may  be  used.

Inclades  bookn,  pamphlets,  leaflet^^  eti*.,  an<)  pfHoiiical  contributions,  in  twofm>upe. 
arranged  alphabetically  by  author  or  title.  The  first  group  contains  the  titles  of 
books  for  which  printed  catalo^e  cards  are  issued  by  the  Librar}*  of  Congreee,  and 
other  similar  works.  The  second  irroup  includes  the  titles  of  pamphlets,  leaflets, 
and  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  all  other  productions  regit«tered  under  the 
legal  designation  "book."  The  titles  included  in  the  second  group  will  be  printe<i 
in  the  last  number  of  the  CATAiiOTirK  of  each  month. 

The  number  in  heavy-faced  type  at  the  end  of  a  title  at  the  right  is  a  citation  number 
by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  index  to  this  title.  Thus  in  the  index 
under  the  heading  "Aschehoug  (H.)  &  co."  will  be  found  the  number  3394.  A 
search  for  this  number  in  the  catalo;rne  lo  -ate^j  the  title.  Thi«  numl)er  is  used 
instead  of  a  page  number. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  a  title  at  the  left  and  immediately  al>ove  the  copyright 
notice  is  the  Library  of  Congrett  order  nnmber  for  the  printed  card  for  thie  title. 
Thus  at  the  end  of  the  title  ** Allen,  John  Kermott.  Sizes  of  flow  and  return 
gteam  mains,"  is  the  number  7-8247,  indicating  that  printed  cards  will  he  sup- 
plied by  the  Library  of  Couirress,  and  that  in  ordering  furh  car<l«  this  nnmlier 
may  be  use^l.

CLASS  A.  BOOKS 

Incladee  books,  pamphlets,  leaflets,  etc.,  and  periodical  contribntionSj  in  twofnx>np«. 
arranged  alphabetically  by  author  or  title.  The  iirst  group  contains  the  titles  of 
books  for  which  printed  catalogue  cards  are  issued  by  the  Library  of  Congrew,  and 
other  similar  works.  The  second  group  includes  the  titles  of  pamphlets,  leaflets, 
and  contributions  to  periodicals,  aud  all  other  productions  registered  umler  the 
l^^al  designation  *'  book.''  The  titles  included  in  the  second  group  will  be  printed 
in  the  last  number  of  the  Catalogue  of  each  month. 

The  number  in  heavy-faced  type  at  the  end  of  a  title  at  the  right  is  a  citation  number 
by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  index  to  this  title.  Thus  in  the  index 
under  the  heading  Macmillan  co.  will  be  found  the  number  3509.  A  search  for 
this  number  in  the  catalogue  locates  the  title.  This  number  is  used  instead  of 
a  page  number. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  a  title  at  the  left  and  immediately  above  the  copyright 
notice  is  the  Library  of  Congress  order  nmiiber  for  the  printed  eard  for  thie  title. 
Thus  at  the  end  of  the  title  ''Bailey  (Liberty  Hvde),  Cyclopedia  of  American 
Agriculture,"  is  the  number  7-8729,  indicating  that  printed  cards  will  be  snp- 
plied  by  the  Library  of  Congress,  and  that  in  ordering  such  cards  this  number 
may  be  usetl.

CLASS  A.  BOOKS 

iDcIndes  tx>oks,  pamphlets,  leaflets,  etc.,  and  periodical  contrlbntions,  in  two 
groups,  arranged  alphabetically  by  author  or  title.  The  first  group  contains 
the  titles  of  books  for  which  printed  catalogue  cards  are  Issued  by  the  Library 
of  Ck>ngress,  and  other  similar  works.  The  second  group  indudes  the  titles 
of  pamphlets,  leaflets,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  all  Other  produc- 
tions registered  under  the  legal  designation  "  book ;  '*  in  this  group  are  in- 
cluded also  titles  of  State  and  U.  S.  court  reports,  directories,  herd  books, 
and  proceedings  of  societies.  The  titles  included  in  the  second  group  will  be 
printed  in  the  last  number  of  the  Catalogue  of  each  month. 

The  number  in  hedvy-faced  type  at  the  end  of  a  title  at  the  right  is  a  citation 
iiuiul>er  by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  index  to  this  title.  Thus 
in  the  index  under  the  heading  of  '*  Abbott,  Lyman.  Christ's  secret  of  happi- 
ness '*  will  be  found  the  number  3592.  A  search  for  this  number  in  the  cata- 
logue locates  the  title.    This  number  is  used  instead  of  a  page  number. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  a  title  at  the  left  and  immediately  above  the  copyright 
notice  is  the  Library  of  Congress  order  nnmber  for  the  printed  card  for  this 
title.  Thus  at  the  end  of  the  title  **  Abbott,  Lyman.  Christ's  secret  of  happi- 
ness "  is  the  number  7-10562,  indicating  that  printed  cards  will  be  supplied  by 
the  Library  of  Congress,  and  that  in  ordering  such  cards  this  nmnber  may  be 
used.

BOOKS;  DRAMATIC  COMPOSITION^ ;  MAPS  AND

'"■•.■-**■■■•. 

CiiAss  A,  Books 

Includes  books,  pamphlets,  leaflets,  etc.,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  in 
two  groups,  arranged  alphabetically  by  author  or,  if  anonymous,  by 
title.  The  first  group  contains  the  titles  of  books  for  which  printed 
catalogue  cards  are  issued  by  the  Library  of  Congress,  including  the 
titles  of  foreign  books  registered  under  the  act  of  March  3,  1905,  for 
ad  interim  protection.  The  second  group  contains  the  titles  of  pam- 
phlets, leaflets,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  all  other  produc- 
tions registered  under  the  legal  designation  "book,"  including  reports  of 
court  decisions,  proceedings  of  societies,  and  directories,  herdbooks,  etc. 
The  titles  included  in  the  second  group  will  be  printed  in  the  last  weekly 
number  of  the  Catai^ogus  for  each  month. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  each  title,  after  the  notice  of  copyright,  is  a 
citation  number  by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  mdex  to 
this  title. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  a  title  and  immediately  above  the  copyright 
notice,  printed  with  a  hyphen,  e.  g.  7-S729^  is  the  JAbniy  of  Congr< 
order  nmnber  for  the  printed  card  for  this  title.

Books.    Gboitp  2 

Pamphlets,  leaflets,  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  serial  publications, 
including  reports  of  court  decisions,  directories,  herdbooks,  etc.,  are  con- 
tained in  Group  2.  To  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small 
alphabets,  the  titles  in  this  group  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  of 
Part  I.  for  each  month,  thus  reducing  the  alphabetical  indexes  to  12  for 
each  year. 


Dramatic  Compositions,  and  Maps  and  Charts 

The  titles  for  entries  made  in  Class  "D,"  Dramatic  Compositions,  and 
Class  "E,"  Maps  and  Charts,  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  for  each 
month  to  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small  alphabets. 


Index  to  Pabt  1.    Books 


This  index  contains  the  names  of  copyrisiit  proprietors  of  books,  the 
titles  of  which  are  printed  in  this  number  of  the  Cataijoguc  The  index 
in  the  last  number  of  the  Catauogu£  of  each  month  will  contain  in  one 
alphabet,  in  addition  to  the  names  of  copyright  proprietors  of  books, 
dramas,  and  maps,  the  titles  of  which  have  appeared  during  the  month, 
the  names  also  of  the  authors  of  books  (except  authors  of  leaflets  and 
contributions  to  periodicals),  the  titles  of  anonymous  books  (except  titles 
of  anonymous  leaflets  and  contributions  to  periodicals),  and  the  names  of 
the  authors  of  dramatic  compositions.

BOOKS;  DSAMAJIC  COMPOSITIONS;  MAPS  AND 

GHAETS 

Class  A,  Books 

Includes  books,  pamphlets,  leaflets,  etc.,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  in 
two  groups,  arranged  alphabetically  by  author  or,  if  anonymous,  by 
title.  The  first  group  contains  the  titles  of  books  for  which  printed 
catalogue  cards  are  issued  by  the  Library  of  Congress,  including  the 
titles  of  foreign  books  registered  under  die  act  of  March  3,  1905,  for 
ad  interim  protection.  The  second  group  contains  the  titles  of  pam- 
phlets, leaflets,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  all  other  produc- 
tions registered  under  the  legal  designation  **book,"  including  reports  of 
court  decisions,  proceedings  of  societies,  and  directories,  herdbooks,  etc 
The  titles  included  in  the  second  group  will  be  printed  in  the  last  weekly 
number  of  the  Catalogue  for  each  month. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  each  title,  after  the  notice  of  copyright,  is  a 
citation  number  by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  mdex  to 
this  title. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  a  title  and  immediately  above  the  copyright 
notice,  printed  with  a  hyphen,  e.  g,  7-8729,  is  the  Library  of  Consri 
order  number  for  the  printed  card  for  thia  title.

Books.    Geoup  2 

Pamphlets,  leaflets,  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  serial  publications, 
including  reports  of  court  decisions,  directories,  herdbooks,  etc.,  are  con- 
tained in  Group  2.  To  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small 
alphabets,  the  titles  in  this  group  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  of 
Part  I.  for  each  month,  thus  reducing  the  alphabetical  indexes  to  12  for 
each  year. 

Dramatic  Compositions,  and  Maps  and  Chabts 

The  titles  for  entries  made  in  Class  "D,"  Dramatic  Compositions,  and 
Class  "E,"  Maps  and  Charts,  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  for  each 
month  to  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small  alphabets. 


Index  to  Part  1.     Books 


This  index  contains  the  names  of  copyright  proprietors  of  books,  the 
titles  of  which  are  printed  in  this  number  of  the  Catalogue.  The  index 
in  the  last  number  of  the  Catalogue  of  each  month  will  contain  in  one 
alphabet,  in  addition  to  the  names  of  copyright  proprietors  of  books, 
dramas,  and  maps,  the  titles  of  which  have  appeared  during  the  month, 
the  names  also  of  the  authors  of  books  (except  authors  of  leaflets  and 
contributions  to  periodicals),  the  titles  of  anonymous  books  (except  titles 
of  anonymous  leaflets  and  contributions  to  periodicals),  and  the  names  of 
the  authors  of  dramatic  compositions.

BOOKS;  DRAMATIC  COMPOSITIONS;  MAPS  AND

» 

Class  A,  Books 

Includes  books,  pamphlets,  leaflets,  etc.,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  in 
two  groups,  arranged  alphabetically  by  author  or,  if  anonymous,  by 
title.  The  first  group  contains  the  titles  of  books  for  which  printed 
catalogue  cards  are  issued  by  the  Library  of  Congress,  tncludina;  the 
titles  of  foreign  books  registered  under  the  act  of  March  3,  190S,  for 
ad  interim  protection.  The  second  group  contains  the  titles  of  pam- 
phlets, leaflets,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  all  other  produc- 
tions registered  under  the  legal  designation  "book,"  including  reports  of 
court  decisions,  proceedings  of  societies,  and  directories,  herdbooks,  etc. 
The  titles  included  in  the  second  group  will  be  printed  in  the  last  weekly 
number  of  the  Catai/)cus  for  each  month. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  each  title,  after  the  notice  of  copyright,  is  a 
citation  number  by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  mdex  to 
this  title. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  a  title  and  immediately  above  the  copyright 
notice,  printed  with  a  hyphen,  e,  2.  7-12989,  is  the  Library  of  Congress 
order  number  for  tiie  printed  card  For  this  title.

Books.    Group  2 

Pamphlets,  leaflets,  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  serial  publications, 
including  reports  of  court  decisions,  directories,  herdbooks,  etc.,  are  con- 
tained in  Group  2.  To  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small 
alphabets,  the  titles  in  this  group  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  of 
Part  I.  for  each  month,  thus  reducing  the  alphabetical  indexes  to  12  for 
each  year. 

Dramatic  Compositions,  and  Maps  and  Charts 

The  titles  for  entries  made  in  Class  "D,"  Dramatic  Compositions,  and 
Class  "E,"  Maps  and  Charts,  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  for  each 
month  to  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small  alphabets. 


Ikd;bz  to  Past  1.    Books 


This  index  contains  the  names  of  copyright  proprietors  of  books,  the 
titles  of  which  are  printed  in  this  number  of  the  Cataloguc  The  index 
in  the  last  number  of  the  CATALOcue  of  each  month  will  contain  in  one 
alphabet,  in  addition  to  the  names  of  copyright  proprietors  of  books, 
dramas,  and  maps,  the  titles  of  which  have  appeared  during  the  month, 
the  names  also  of  the  authors  of  books  (except  authors  of  leaflets  and 
contributions  to  periodicals),  the  titles  of  anonymous  books  (except  titles 
of  anonymous  leaflets  and  contributions  to  periodicals),  and  the  names  of 
the  authors  of  dramatic  compositions.

CLASS  A,  BOOKS. 

Includes  books,  pamphlets,  leaflets,  eta,  and  oontributionfl  to  periodicals,  in  two 
groDpe,  arranged  alphabetically  by  author  or,  if  anonymous,  by  title.  The 
first  group  contains  the  titles  of  books  for  which  printed  catalogue  cards  are 
issoed  by  the  Library  of  Congress,  including  the  titles  of  foreign  books  regis- 
tered under  the  act  of  March  3,  1905.  for  ad  interim  protection.  The  second 
group  contains  the  titles  of  pamphlets,  leaflets,  and  contributions  to  periodi- 
cals, and  all  other  productions  registered  under  the  legal  designation  '*  book," 
including  reports  of  court  dei'isions,  prm-eedings  of  so<*ietie8,  and  directories, 
herdbooks,  etc.  The  titles  included  in  the  second  group  will  be  printed  in  the 
last  weekly  number  of  the  Catalogue  for  each  month. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  each  title,  after  the  notice  of  copyright,  is  a  citation 
number  by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  index  to  this  title. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  a  title  and  immediately  above  the  copyright  notice, 
printed  with  a  hyphen,  e.  g.  7-8729,  is  the  Library  of  Congress  order  namber  for  the 
priated  card  for  this  title. 

GROUP    I. 


Allen,  Alfred  H.

the  author  and  Arnold  Rowsby  Tankard,  v.  2,  part  B.  Phila- 
delphia, P.  Blakiston'a  son  &  co.,  1907. 

xli,  547  p.     23cm.  [4901

BOOKS.     GROUP  2. 

Pamphlets,  leaflets,  contributions  to  periodicals,  etc.  These  titles  will  be  cumu- 
lated throughout  the  month  and  will  be  printe<l  in  the  last  uumi>er  of  the 
Catalogue  of  each  month.  When  the  author  is  also  the  copyright  proprietor 
of  a  given  book,  an  asterisic  appears  after  the  author's  name ;  when  the  author 
and  copyright  proprietor  are  different,  a  separate  statement  is  made  at  the 
end  of  the  title,  giving  the  proprietor  and  his  address.  No  stateiuent  is  made 
as  to  receipt  of  copies  when  the  two  copies  ha^e  been  rei-eived  on  the  day  of 
entry.

CLASS  D.  DRAMATIC  COMPOSITIONS 


Arranged  alphabetically  t^  titles.  For  Index  of  proprietors  and  authors  see  end  of 
Catalooui. 

The  titles  of  Dramatic  Compositions  are  cumulated  for  one  month  and  printed  in  the 
last  issue  of  the  Catalooub  of  each  month. 

To  save  space  the  copyrieht  notice  after  each  title  is  abbreTiated.  The  statement  as  to 
receipt  of  2  copies  is  always  omitted  when  the  2  copies  hare  been  received  on  the  day 
of  entry.  Thus  the  notice  after  the  first  title  if  printed  in  full  would  read,  **  Copy- 
rii^ted  bv  Jean  Bumside,  New  Tork,  N.  Y.  Class  D,  XXc,  no.  10253,  Mar.  26,  1007; 
2  typewritten  copies  received  Feb.  27,  1007."

Class  A,  Books 

Includes  books,  pamphlets,  leaflets,  etc.,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  in 
two  groups,  arranged  alphabetically  by  author  or,  if  anonymous,  by 
title.  The  first  group  contains  the  titles  of  books  for  which  printed 
catalogue  cards  are  issued  by  the  Library  of  Congress,  including  the 
titles  of  foreign  books  registered  under  the  act  of  March  3,  1905,  for 
ad  interim  protection.  The  second  group  contains  the  titles  of  pam- 
phlets, leaflets,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  all  other  produc- 
tions registered  under  the  legal  designation  ''book,"  including  reports  of 
court  decisions,  proceedings  of  societies,  and  directories,  herdbooks,  etc. 
The  titles  included  in  the  second  group  will  be  printed  in  the  last  weekly 
number  of  the  Catalogue  for  each  month. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  each  title,  after  the  notice  of  copyright,  is  a 
citation  number  by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  mdex  to 
this  title. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  a  title  and  immediately  above  the  copyright 
notice,  printed  with  a  hyphen,  e.  g.  7-15128,  is  the  Library  of  Congress 
order  number  for  the  printed  card  for  this  title.

Books.    Gboxjp  2 

Pamphlets,  leaflets,  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  serial  publications, 
including  reports  of  court  decisions,  directories,  herdbooks,  etc.,  are  con- 
tained in  Group  2.  To  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small 
alphabets,  the  titles  in  this  group  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  of 
Part  I.  for  each  month,  thus  reducing  the  alphabetical  indexes  to  12  for 
eadi  year. 


Dramatic  Compositions,  and  Maps  and  Charts 

The  titles  for  entries  made  in  Class  "D,"  Dramatic  Compositions,  and 
Class  "E,"  Maps  and  Charts,  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  for  each 
month  to  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small  alphabets.

Class  A,  Books 

Includes  books,  pamphlets,  leaflets,  etc.,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  in 
two  groups,  arranged  alphabetically  by  author  or,  if  anonymous,  b>' 
title.  The  first  group  contains  the  titles  of  books  for  which  printed 
catalogue  cards  are  issued  by  the  Library  of '  Congress,  including  the 
titles  of  foreign  books  registered  under  the  act  of  March  3,  190S,  for 
ad  interim  protection.  The  second  group  contains  the  titles  of  pam- 
phlets, leaflets,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  all  other  produc- 
tions registered  under  the  legal  designation  "book,"  including  reports  of 
court  decisions,  proceedings  of  societies,  and  directories,  herdbooks,  etc. 
The  titles  included  in  the  second  group  will  be  printed  in  the  last  weekly 
number  of  the  Catalogue  for  each  month. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  each  title,  after  the  notice  of  copyright,  is  a 
citation  number  by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  mdex  to 
this  title. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  a  title  and  immediately  above  the  copyright 
notice,  printed  with  a  hyphen,  e.  g.  7-15629,  is  the  Library  of  Congress 
order  number  for  the  printed  card  for  this  title.

371  p.    20*-. 

"Of  the  essays  forming  this  volume,  some  two  or  three  are  now  pub- 
lished, in  English,  for  the  first  time.  The  remainder  have  appeared,  at

Contents. — ^The  measure  of  the  hours. — Immortality. — The  gods  of

Books.    Group  2 

Pamphlets,  leaflets,  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  serial  publications, 
including  reports  of  court  decisions,  directories,  herdbooks,  etc.,  arc  con- 
tained in  Group  2.  To  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small 
alphabets,  the  titles  in  this  group  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  of 
Part  I.  for  each  month,  thus  reducing  the  alphabetical  indexes  to  12  for 
each  year. 


Dramatic  Compositions,  and  Maps  and  Charts 

The  titles  for  entries  made  in  Class  "D,"  Dramatic  Compositions,  and 
Class  "E,"  Maps  and  Charts,  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  for  each 
month  to  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small  alphabets.

t  - 


Class  A,  Books  , 

Includes  books,  pamphleb^  leailets,  etc.,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  in 
two  groups,  arranged  a]phat)<etically  bv  author  or,  if  anonymous,  by 
title.  The  first  group  contains  the  titles  of  books  for  which  printed 
catalogue  cards  are  issued  by  the  Library  of  Congress,  includtnff  the 
titles  of  foreign  books  registered  under  the  act  of  March  3,  1905,  for 
ad  interim  protection.  The  second  group  contains  the  titles  of  pam- 
phlets, leaflets,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  all  other  produc- 
tions registered  under  the  legal  designation  "book,"  including  reports  of 
court  decisions,  proceedings  of  societies,  and  directories,  herdbooks,  etc. 
The  titles  included  in  the  second  group  will  be  printed  in  the  last  weekly 
number  of  the  Cataixx;uE  for  each  month. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  each  title,  after  the  notice  of  copyri^t,  is  a 
citation  number  by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  mdex  to 
this  title. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  a  title  and  immediately  above  the  copyright 
notice,  printed  with  a  hyphen,  e.  g.,  7-16495,  is  the  Library  of  Congress 
order  number  for  the  printed  card  for  this  title.

Pamphlets,  leaflets,  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  serial  publications, 
including  reports  of  court  decisions,  directories,  herdbooks,  etc,  are  con- 
tained in  Group  2.  To  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small 
alphabets,  the  titles  in  this  group  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  of 
Part  I.  for  each  month,  thus  reducing  the  alphabetical  indexes  to  12  for 
each  year.

CtAss  A,  Books 

Includes  books,  pamphlets,  leaflet^,  ^etc,,.  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  in 
two  groups,  arranged  alphabetically  by  author  or,  if  anonymous,  b>' 
title.  The  first  group  cont:fcins  the  titles  of  books  for  which  printed 
catalogue  cards  are  issued  by  the  Library  of  Congress,  includmg  the 
titles  of  foreign  books  registered  under  the  act  of  March  3,  190$,  for 
ad  interim  protection.  The  second  group  contains  the  titles  of  pam- 
phlets, leaflets,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  all  other  produc- 
tions registered  under  the  legal  designation  "book,'*  including  reports  of 
court  decisions,  proceedings  of  societies,  and  directories,  herdbooks,  etc. 
The  titles  included  in  the  second  group  will  be  printed  in  the  last  weekly 
number  of  the  Catalogue  for  each  month. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  each  title,  after  the  notice  of  copyri^t,  is  a 
citation  number  by  means  of  whidi  reference  is  made  in  the  mdex  to 
this  title. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  a  title  and  immediately  above  the  copyright 
notice,  printed  with  a  hyphen,  e.  g.,  7-15612,  is  the  Library  of  Congresa 
order  munber  for  the  printed  card  for  this  title.

including  reports  of  court  decisions,  directories,  herdbooks,  etc.,  are  con-

alphabets,  the  titles  in  this  group  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  of 
Part  I.  for  each  month,  thus  reducing  the  alphabetical  indexes  to  12  for

BOOKS;  DRAMATIC  COMPOSITIONS;  MAPS  AND  CHARTS. 


CLASS  A,  BOOKS. 

Inclades  books,  pamphlets,  leaflets,  etc.,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  in  two 
groups,  arranged  alphabetically  by  author  or,  if  anonymous,  by  title.  The 
first  group  contains  the  titles  of  books  for  which  printed  catalogue  cards  are 
issued  by  the  Library  of  Congress,  including  the  titles  of  foreign  books  regis- 
tered under  the  act  of  March  3,.  1905^  for  ad  interim  protection.  The  second 
group  contains  the  titles  of  pamphlets,  leaflets,  and  contributions  to  period- 
icals, and  all  other  productions  registered  under  the  le^al  designation  "  book," 
including  reports  of  court  decif^ons,  proceedings  of  societies,  and  directories, 
herdbooks,  etc.  The  titles  included  in  the  seoond  group  will  be  printed  in  the 
last  weekly  number  of  the  Catalogue  for  each  month. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  each  title,  after  the  notice  of  copyright,  is  a  citation 
number  by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  Index  to  this  title. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  a  title  and  inunediately  above  the  copyright  notice, 
printed  with  a  hyphen,  e.  g.  7-8729,  is  the  Libraiy  of  Congress  order  number  for  the 
printed  card  for  this  title. 

GROUP    1 


American  school  of  correspondence  at  Armour  institute  of 
technology,  Chicago. 

Metallurgy;  instruction  paper,  revised  by  Ralph  H.  Sweetser  .  .  . 
Chicago,  111.,  American  school  of  correspondence  [1907].

Pamphlets,  leaflets,  contributions  to  periodicals,  etc  These  titles  will  be  cumu- 
lated throughout  the  month  and  will  be  printed  in  the  last  number  of  the 
Gataijogue  of  each  month.  When  the  author  Is  also  the  copyright  proprietor 
of  a  giY&i  book,  an  asterisk  appears  after  the  author's  name :  when  the  author 
and  copyright  proprietor  are  different,  a  separate  statement  is  made  at  the 
end  of  tlie  title,  giving  the  proprietor  and  his  address.  No  statement  is  made 
as  to  receipt  of  copies  when  the  two  copies  have  been  received  on  the  day  of 
entry.

CLASS  D.  DRAMATIC  COMPOSITIONS 

Arraofred  alphabetically  by  titles.     For  Index  of  proprietors  and  aatbors  see  end  of 

CATALOOrE. 

The  titles  of  Dramatic  Compositions  are  cumulated  for  one  month  and  printed  In  the 
last  issue  of  the  Catalooub  of  each  month. 

To  save  space  the  copyright  notice  after  each  title  Is  abbreviated.  The  statement  as  to 
receipt  of  2  copies  is  always  omitted  when  the  2  copies  have  been  received  on  the  day 
of  entry.  Thus  the  notice  after  the  first  title  if  printed  in  full  would  read,  **  Copy- 
righted by  Sarah  Bernhardt,  Paris,  France.  Class  D,  XXc,  no.  10413,  Apr.  25,  1907; 
2  copies  received  Apr.  25,  1907."

as  to  receipt  of  copies  if  copies  are  recelTed  on  day  of  entry.  Thns  the  notice  after  the

BOOKS;  DRAMATIC  COMPOSITIONS;  MAPS  AND 

CHARTS 


Class  A,  Books 

Includes  books,  pamphlets,  leaflets,  etc.,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  in 
two  groups,  arranged  alphabetically  by  author  or,  if  anonymous,  b5 
title.  The  first  group  contains,  the.  titles  of  books  for  which  printed 
catalogue  cards  are  issued  by  the  Library  of  Congress,  inctudins  the 
titles  of  foreign  books  registered  under  the  act  of  March  3,  190S,  for 
ad  interim  protection.  The  second  group  contains  the  titles  of  pam- 
phlets, leaflets,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  all  other  produc- 
tions registered  under  the  legal  designation  "book,'*  including  reports  of 
court  decisions,  proceedings  of  societies,  and  directories,  herdbooks,  etc. 
The  titles  included  in  the  second  group  will  be  printed  in  the  last  weekly 
number  of  the  Catai«ogu9  for  each  month. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  each  title,  after  the  notice  of  copyright,  is  a 
citation  number  by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  mdex  to 
this  title. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  a  title  and  immediately  above  the  copyright 
notice,  printed  with  a  hyphen,  e,  g.,  7-17400,  is  the  Library  of  Congress 
order  number  for  the  printed  card  for  this  title.

Books.    Group  2 

Pamphlets,  leaflets,  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  serial  publications, 
including  reports  of  court  decisions,  directories,  herdbooks,  etc.,  are  con- 
tained in  Group  2.  To  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small 
alphabets,  the  titles  in  this  group  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  of 
Part  I.  for  each  month,  thus  reducing  the  alphabetical  indexes  to  12  for 
each  year. 


Dramatic  Compositions,  and  Maps  and  Charts 

The  titles  for  entries  made  in  Class  "D,"  Dramatic  Compositions,  and 
Class  "E,"  Maps  and  Charts,  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  for  each 
month  to  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small  alphabets.

BOOKS;  DRAMATie  COMPOSITIONS;  MAPS  AND

Class  A,  Books 

Includes  books,  pamphlets,  leaflets, '  etc.,  arid  contributions  to  periodicals,  in 
two  groups,  arranged  alphabetically  bv  author  or,  if  anonymous,  by 
title.  The  first  group  contains  the  titles  of  books  for  which  printed 
catalogue  cards  are  issued  by  the  Library  of  Congress,  including  the 
titles  of  foreign  books  registered  under  the  act  of  March  3,  1905,  for 
ad  interim  protection.  The  second  group  contains  the  titles  of  pam- 
phlets, leaflets,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  all  other  produc- 
tions registered  under  the  legal  designation  "book,"  including  reports  of 
court  decisions,  proceedings  of  societies,  and  directories,  herdbooks,  etc. 
The  titles  included  in  the  second  group  will  be  printed  in  the  last  weekly 
number  of  the  Catalogue  for  eadi  month. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  each  title,  after  the  notice  of  copyri^t,  is  a 
citation  number  by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  mdex  to 
this  title. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  a  title  and  immediately  above  the  copyright 
notice,  printed  with  a  hyphen,  e.  g.^  7-19479,  is  the  Library  of  Congress 
order  nnmber  for  the  printed  card  for  this  title.

Books.    Group  2 

Pamphlets,  leaflets,  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  serial  publications, 
including  reports  of  court  decisions,  directories,  herdbooks,  etc.,  are  con- 
tained in  Group  2.  To  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small 
alphabets,  the  titles  in  this  group  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  of 
Part  I.  for  each  month,  thus  reducing  the  alphabetical  indexes  to  12  for 
each  year. 


Dramatic  Compositions,  and  Maps  and  Charts 

The  titles  for  entries  made  in  Class  "D,"  Dramatic  Compositions,  and 
Class  "E,"  Maps  and  Charts,  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  for  each 
month  to  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small  alphabets.

CLASS  A.  BOOKS. 

Indades  books,  pamphlets,  leaflets,  eto.rdnd-  contributions  to  periodicals,  in  twa 
groups,  arranged  alphabetically  by  author  or,  if  anonymous,  by  title.  The 
first  group  contains  the  titles  of  books  for  which  printed  catalogue  cards  are 
issued  by  the  Library  of  Congress,  including  the  titles  of  foreign  books  regis- 
tered under  the  act  of  March  3,  1905,  for  ad  interim  protection.  The  second 
group  contains  the  titles  of  pamphlets,  leaflets,  and  contributions  to  period- 
icals, and  all  other  productions  registered  under  the  legal  designation  "  book," 
including  reports  of  court  decisions,  proceedings  of  societies,  and  directories, 
herdbooks,  etc.  The  titles  included  in  the  second  group  will  be  printed  in  the 
last  weekly  number  of  the  Catalogue  for  each  month. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  each  title,  after  the  notice  of  copyright,  is  a  citation 
number  by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  index  to  this  title. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  a  title  and  immediately  above  the  copyright  notice, 
printed  with  a  hyphen,  e.  g,  7-8729,  Is  the  Library  of  Congress  order  number 
for  the  printed  card  for  this  title.

end  of  the  title,  giving  the  proprietor  and  his  addres&  No  statement  is  made

CLASS  D.  DRAMATIC  COMPOSITIONS 

ArraDged  alphabetically  by  titles.    For  Index  of  proprietom  and  authore  see  end  of  Catalogue. 

The  titles  of  Dramatic  Compositions  are  cumulated  for  one  month  and  printed  In  the  last  Issue  of 

the  Catalogue  of  each  month. 

To  save  space  the  copyright  notice  after  each  title  is  abbreviated.  The  statement  as  to  receipt  of 
2  coplex  is  always  omitted  when  the  2  copies  have  been  received  on  the  day  of  entry.  Thus  the 
notice  after  the  tint  title  if  printed  in  full  would  read,  "Copyrighted  by  Luis  de  Larray  Ricardo 
Fem&ndez  Blanco,  Madrid,  Spain.  Class  D,  XXc,  no.  10659,  May  29. 1907;*  2  copies  received  May  29, 
1907."

CLASS  E.  MAPS  AND  CHARTS 

Arranged  alphabetically  by  the  subject  word  of  each  title.    For  index  of  proprietors,  Bee  end  of 

Catalogue. 

The  titles  of  Maps  and  Charts  are  cumulated  for  one  month  and  printed  in  the  last  issue  of  the  cat- 
alogue of  each  month. 

To  save  space  the  copyright  notice  after  each  title  is  abbreviated:  no  statement  it*  made  as  to 
receipt  of  copies  if  cop  es  are  received  on  day  of  entrj*.  Thus  the  notice  after  the  first  title,  if  printed 
in  full,  would  read:  "Copyright  by  Western  publishing  house,  Chicago.  III.  Class  E.  XXc.  no.  10frl8, 
Apr.  8, 1907;  2  copies  received  May  18, 1907."

VOLUME  INDEX 


PART  1.     BOOKS,  DRAMATIC  COMPOSITIONS, 

MAPS  AND  CHARTS 


This  index  contains  in  one  alphabet,  in  addition  to  the  names  of  copyright  proprietors 
of  books,  dramas,  and  maps,  the  names  also  of  the  authors  of  books  (except  authors  of 
leaflets  and  contributions  to  periodicals),  the  titles  of  anonymous  books  (except  titles 
of  anonymous  leaflets  and  contributions  to  periodicals),  the  names  of  the  authors  of 
dramatic  compositions,  the  titles  of  dramatic  compositions,  and  the  subject  words  of 
the  titles  of  maps. 

Where  there  is  a  printed  card  its  number  follows  in  parentheses  the  author  or  pro- 
prietor reference.  The  figures  at  the  right,  not  in  parentheses,  refer  to  the  citation 
numbexB  following  the  titles  in  the  Catalogue.
